description: Steelman: Google ADK native ArtifactService + load_artifacts tool for on-demand document loading (Context Tax).
quality: PASS (all 3 doc-turn substring checks on the smoke run)
finding: ADK's load_artifacts IS a genuine ephemeral-attachment equivalent for the document (session inspection: report text 0x in standing history vs 1x for default). It does NOT close the context-tax gap because the scenario's cost is dominated by the ~8k-token base64 chart, which accumulates in ADK history (ADK lacks Colmena's binary scrubber, Mechanism B), and load_artifacts adds a reload round-trip each doc-turn that re-sends the un-scrubbed history. ADK matches 1 of Colmena's 2 context mechanisms (ephemeral attachment) but not the other (binary scrubbing). Result: 468k vs 440k default, both ~11-12x Colmena's 39k. The gap is from ADK lacking a binary-scrubbing default, not from under-using its document handling.

doc_in_standing_history.note: 0 => load_artifacts is ephemeral (doc not pinned to session); matches Colmena Mechanism A

chart_base64_in_standing_history.note: >0 => chart accumulates; ADK has no binary scrubber (Colmena Mechanism B)
